texto: Beyond the immediate risks to her health and the health of her baby, when a woman chooses c-section, she decreases the chance that she will be able to get pregnant again and increases the chance that if she does get pregnant, the pregnancy will occur outside the uterus, a situation that never results in a live baby and is life-threatening to the woman. Furthermore, the risk of having an unexplained stillbirth doubles when a woman has had a previous c-section.
explicacao: Citação de Marsden Wagner sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Beyond the immediate risks to her health and the health of her baby, when a woman chooses c-secti…” articula vida, birth, c-section em registro de vida, com relevância para contextos como c-section e pregnancy.

Furthermore, the risk of having an unexplained stillbirth doubles when a woman has had a previous c-section.

texto: ...the total number of pregnancies in which powerful and dangerous drugs are used is 60 percent, or nearly two-thirds of all births. It is rediculous to think that two-thirds of American women have such lousy uteruses that they must be whipped into shape with drugs in order to have babies.
